text.title: Error Handling in VUI Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules anchoring the error-handling-in-vui methodology, each grounded in a source.

rule.statement: Recovery MUST follow the three-rung ladder: rung 1 brief re-ask, rung 2 constructive re-ask with 2+ example phrases, rung 3 escalation (visual / DTMF / human).
rule.rationale: Two rungs of voice retry + escalation is the proven pattern; deeper ladders frustrate users.

rule.statement: Each rung MUST vary the prompt (rephrase + add scaffolding); verbatim repeat is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Verbatim repeat rarely fixes the underlying confusion and sounds robotic.

rule.statement: Rung 2 MUST include >=2 concrete example phrases the user could say.
rule.rationale: Examples scaffold first-time users + non-natives faster than abstract instruction.

rule.statement: Rung 3 MUST escalate to a non-voice channel (visual / DTMF / human); a third voice re-ask is forbidden.
rule.rationale: After 2 failed re-asks, voice is unlikely to succeed; channel change is required.

rule.statement: No-input (silent) timeouts MUST have their own ladder distinct from misrecognition; conflating them is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Silent timeout suggests user not present or unsure; misrecognition suggests user spoke. Different prompts apply.

rule.statement: If the Skip-If preconditions hold, the methodology MUST be skipped and the caller routed to the linked alternative.
rule.rationale: Applying this machinery to the wrong workflow burns tokens without producing a usable artefact.
